relations: The egg stage is the beginning of the butterfly's life cycle
The larva stage is a critical part of the butterfly's transformation process
The pupa stage is a crucial part of the butterfly's life cycle, leading to its emergence as an adult
The butterfly is the final stage in the life cycle that begins with the caterpillar
Metamorphosis results in the transformation of the caterpillar into a butterfly
The larva stage follows the egg stage in the butterfly's life cycle, with the caterpillar emerging from the egg
Caterpillars represent the larva stage in the butterfly's life cycle, focusing on feeding and growth
After completing its growth, the caterpillar enters the pupa stage, marking its transformation towards becoming an adult butterfly
The caterpillar forms the chrysalis around itself to protect during the pupa stage of its life cycle
Inside the chrysalis, the caterpillar undergoes metamorphosis
Butterflies feed on nectar from flowers
Butterflies contribute to pollination by transferring pollen
Female butterflies lay their eggs on host plants
Butterflies serve as a food source for birds
Butterflies are consumed by reptiles, indicating a predator-prey relationship
Butterflies contribute to the overall biodiversity by being part of the food chain involving other insects
Adult butterflies feed on nectar as their primary food source
Female butterflies specifically choose host plants to lay their eggs
